name: Pre/Post Install Hooks for Migrations and Setup
description: Use Helm lifecycle hooks to run database migrations, seed data, send notifications, and perform cleanup tasks during chart install and upgrade operations.

steps.title: Create a pre-install hook for database migration
steps.description: Define a Job that runs before the main application is deployed.
steps.command:
cat > myapp/templates/pre-install-migration.yaml << 'EOF'
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}-migrate
  annotations:
    "helm.sh/hook": pre-install,pre-upgrade
    "helm.sh/hook-weight": "0"
    "helm.sh/hook-delete-policy": hook-succeeded
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: migrate
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          command: ["npm", "run", "db:migrate"]
          envFrom:
            - secretRef:
                name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}-db-secret
EOF
steps.tip: hook-weight controls execution order when multiple hooks exist. Lower weights run first.

steps.title: Add a post-install notification hook
steps.description: Send a Slack notification after a successful deployment.
steps.command:
cat > myapp/templates/post-install-notify.yaml << 'EOF'
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}-notify
  annotations:
    "helm.sh/hook": post-install,post-upgrade
    "helm.sh/hook-weight": "10"
    "helm.sh/hook-delete-policy": hook-succeeded,hook-failed
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
        - name: notify
          image: curlimages/curl:latest
          command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -X POST "$SLACK_WEBHOOK" -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
                -d '{"text": "Deployed {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }} v{{ .Chart.AppVersion }}"}'
          env:
            - name: SLACK_WEBHOOK
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: slack-webhook
                  key: url
EOF

steps.title: Verify hook annotations
steps.description: Render templates to confirm hooks are correctly annotated.

steps.title: Test hooks with a dry run
steps.description: Simulate the install to see hook execution order.
steps.warning: Hooks with 'hook-delete-policy: hook-succeeded' are automatically deleted after success. Use 'before-hook-creation' to delete the previous hook resource before creating a new one on upgrade.

steps.title: Debug a failed hook
steps.description: Inspect hook Job logs when a deployment is stuck.
steps.command: kubectl get jobs -n production -l 'app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm' && kubectl logs job/<release>-migrate -n production

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Hooks Debugging Workflow
set -euo pipefail

RELEASE=${1:?"Usage: $0 <release-name> <namespace>"}
NAMESPACE=${2:-"default"}

echo "=== Hook Jobs ==="
kubectl get jobs -n "$NAMESPACE" -l "app.kubernetes.io/instance=$RELEASE" \
  -o custom-columns='NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.conditions[0].type,COMPLETIONS:.status.succeeded'

echo ""
echo "=== Hook Events ==="
kubectl get events -n "$NAMESPACE" --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' \
  --field-selector reason=Created | grep -i "$RELEASE" | tail -10

echo ""
echo "=== Failed Hook Logs ==="
for job in $(kubectl get jobs -n "$NAMESPACE" -l "app.kubernetes.io/instance=$RELEASE" \
  -o jsonpath='{.items[?(@.status.failed)].metadata.name}'); do
  echo "--- Logs for $job ---"
  kubectl logs "job/$job" -n "$NAMESPACE" --tail=50
done

echo ""
echo "=== Tips ==="
echo "Force delete stuck hooks: kubectl delete job <job-name> -n $NAMESPACE"
echo "Skip hooks on upgrade:    helm upgrade $RELEASE <chart> --no-hooks"

faq.question: What hook types are available in Helm?
faq.answer: Helm supports: pre-install, post-install, pre-delete, post-delete, pre-upgrade, post-upgrade, pre-rollback, post-rollback, and test. Each runs at the corresponding point in the release lifecycle.

faq.question: What happens if a pre-install hook fails?
faq.answer: If a pre-install or pre-upgrade hook fails, Helm aborts the entire operation. The release status is set to 'failed' and the main resources are not deployed. Fix the hook and retry the install.

faq.question: How do I control the order of multiple hooks?
faq.answer: Use the 'helm.sh/hook-weight' annotation. Hooks are sorted by weight in ascending order and executed sequentially. Use negative weights to run hooks before those with weight 0.
